name: Great Reset Financial
description: The Great Reset Financial is a conspiracy theory alleging a secret plan by global elites, often involving figures in finance, politics, and international organizations, to intentionally restructure the global economy and financial system for their own benefit. Proponents claim this involves dismantling existing capitalist structures, eroding national sovereignty, and concentrating wealth and power in the hands of a select few. They often point to the World Economic Forum's (WEF) Great Reset initiative, launched in 2020, as evidence of this agenda. The WEF's stated goals include addressing climate change, reducing inequality, and leveraging technology for sustainable development. However, conspiracy theorists interpret these goals as a smokescreen for a more sinister agenda involving social engineering, digital surveillance, and the elimination of private property. Evidence cited often includes WEF publications, speeches by prominent figures, and perceived correlations between global events (like the COVID-19 pandemic) and the alleged reset agenda. Mainstream perspectives view the Great Reset as a legitimate effort to address global challenges through international cooperation and sustainable development. Academics and journalists argue that the conspiracy theory misinterprets the WEF's intentions and exaggerates its influence, often relying on selective quotes and misrepresentations. Critics also point out the lack of concrete evidence supporting the claim of a coordinated, secret plan, and highlight the diversity of opinions and interests within the WEF and other organizations implicated in the theory. The theory often intertwines with existing anxieties about globalization, technological advancements, and perceived erosion of individual freedoms.
subtitle: Alleged global elite financial restructuring plan

claims.claim: Global elites are planning a financial reset to consolidate power.
claims.evidence: WEF publications, statements by global leaders, perceived manipulation of global events.
claims.criticism: These are misinterpreted as evidence of a nefarious plan; the WEF promotes sustainable development and international cooperation.

claims.claim: The COVID-19 pandemic was engineered to facilitate the Great Reset.
claims.evidence: Timing of the pandemic relative to the WEF's Great Reset initiative, perceived overreach of government responses.
claims.criticism: The pandemic's origins are scientifically explained; government responses were aimed at public health and economic stability.

claims.claim: The Great Reset will lead to the elimination of private property and individual freedoms.
claims.evidence: Interpretations of WEF statements regarding stakeholder capitalism and sustainable development.
claims.criticism: These interpretations are based on misunderstandings and selective quoting; the WEF promotes responsible business practices, not the abolition of private property.

keyFigures.name: Klaus Schwab
keyFigures.role: Founder and Executive Chairman of the World Economic Forum (often portrayed as a key architect of the Great Reset)

keyFigures.name: Bill Gates
keyFigures.role: Philanthropist and technology entrepreneur (often accused of being involved in the Great Reset agenda)

keyFigures.name: Naomi Klein
keyFigures.role: Social activist and author (critic of neoliberalism and capitalism, whose work is sometimes misinterpreted as supporting the Great Reset conspiracy)

keyFigures.name: Steven Poole
keyFigures.role: Journalist and author (criticizes the Great Reset conspiracy theory for its lack of evidence and its reliance on misinterpretations)

mythologyConnections: Apocalyptic narratives of societal collapse and rebirth

timeline.event: Klaus Schwab founds the World Economic Forum.
The World Economic Forum announces the Great Reset initiative in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.
The Great Reset Financial conspiracy theory gains traction online and in various media outlets.

sources.author: Klaus Schwab and Thierry Malleret
sources.perspective: proponent (of the WEF's Great Reset initiative)
sources.title: Klaus Schwab and Thierry Malleret

sources.author: World Economic Forum

sources.perspective: proponent (of the WEF's Great Reset initiative)

sources.title: Steven Poole
